description: Information on customer accounts registered with Piespace. Each account represents a new organization signing up for on-demand pies.

db.description: Some example data for you to play around with.
db.name: Sample Database
db.caveats: You probably don't want to use this for your business-critical analyses, but hey, it's your world, we're just living in it.
db.points_of_interest: You can find all sorts of different joinable tables ranging from products to people to reviews here.

name: ACCOUNTS
caveats: Piespace’s business operates with a two week trial period. If you see that “Canceled At” is null then that account is still happily paying for their pies.
points_of_interest: Is it? We’ll let you be the judge of that.

description: Piespace does some anonymous analytics tracking on how users interact with their platform. They’ve only had time to implement a few events, but you know how it is. Pies come first.

db.description: Some example data for you to play around with.

db.caveats: You probably don't want to use this for your business-critical analyses, but hey, it's your world, we're just living in it.
db.points_of_interest: You can find all sorts of different joinable tables ranging from products to people to reviews here.

name: ANALYTIC_EVENTS
caveats: Piespace has cracked time travel, so keep in mind that some events may have already happened in the future.
points_of_interest: Is it? We’ll let you be the judge of that.

description: With each order of pies sent out, Piespace includes a place for customers to submit feedback and review their order.

name: FEEDBACK
caveats: Not every account feels inclined to submit feedback. That’s cool. There’s still quite a few responses here.
points_of_interest: Is it? We’ll let you be the judge of that.

description: Confirmed payments from Piespace’s customers. Most accounts pay for their pie subscription on a monthly basis.

caveats: You can group by “Account ID” to see all the payments from an account and unveil information like total amount paid to date.
